text.title: Cost-Per-DAU Defense Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every cost-per-DAU defense report must enforce. HARD rules — finance review will reject the report otherwise.

rule.statement: cost_per_dau_usd is computed from measured inference cost ledger over the last 30 days divided by measured DAU. Modeled estimates, projections, or extrapolations are rejected.
rule.rationale: Modeled numbers collapse under scrutiny; measured numbers survive because they cite their source rows. The cheapest model per token is often the most expensive per result, so per-DAU must be measured per-call not per-model.

rule.statement: Exactly three top cost drivers are reported, each with percentage contribution. The three must sum to ≥70% of total cost.
rule.rationale: More than three drivers dilute the narrative; fewer hides the underlying mechanism. Three forces the author to do the ranking work.

rule.statement: The 90-day plan names a single accountable owner and a numeric target cost_per_dau_usd. No 'team' / 'we' / 'aspirational'.
rule.rationale: An unowned target is a wish; a named owner + number is a commitment finance can hold.

rule.statement: At least one peer benchmark is cited (vendor pricing page URL, public earnings disclosure, named blog post). 'Industry standard' phrasing without a citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Cost claims without a peer reference cannot be falsified. Citations let finance verify the benchmark independently.

rule.statement: The plan horizon is exactly 90 days. Plans with milestones beyond day 90 are rejected; longer plans split into multiple defense reports.
rule.rationale: Open-ended plans never finish. A 90-day fence forces specific, deliverable interventions and a measurable post-mortem.
